sections.title: Starting the adventure
Talking to Burntmeat without meeting the requirements

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Oh, it you again. I bin thinking 'bout you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I probably won't feel happy for knowing the answer to this, but why have you been thinking about me?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Ohhh, Burntmeat not finished thinking yet. Maybe next time you come here I tell you.

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Oh, it you again. I bin thinking 'bout you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I probably won't feel happy for knowing the answer to this, but why have you been thinking about me?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Well, Burntmeat need big important job doing, and you bin helpful before. You fetch Burntmeat tasty human for stew that time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you want now?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: You remember you come in here asking me questions about goutweed, yah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, you told me trolls had picked it all until there wasn't any left.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Yah. But Burntmeat is hearing where goutweed is growing again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh? How did you hear that?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Well, I was cooking dis adventurer...

lines.speaker: Adventurer
lines.text: You like my armour? If you let me go, I'll let you keep it!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Nah, silly red metal just hurt troll teeth.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Huh? What dis? You got goutweed here!

lines.speaker: Adventurer
lines.text: You can have that too! Just let me go!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Only place find goutweed is my storeroom. You bin stealing?

lines.speaker: Adventurer
lines.text: No! I-I- I grew that myself!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: What say? Goutweed not grow now. Trolls pick it all.

lines.speaker: Adventurer
lines.text: No, no; I grew it.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Humans grow goutweed? You lying to Burntmeat!

lines.speaker: Adventurer
lines.text: You can get the gout tuber from Tai Bwo Wannai Village. Go there and ask Murcaily. Then you plant it in a farming patch.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Mmmm. Burntmeat think that sound good. But Burntmeat cook you first.

lines.speaker: Adventurer
lines.text: Oh b...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You killed that poor chap! What had he ever done to you?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Well, he give me mighty sore tummy later. I think I forget to wash his feet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anyway, why did you tell me about all that?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Ah! Der man I cooked, he say humans growing goutweed again. Now, if humans growing goutweed, maybe trolls can grow goutweed too, yah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You want to become a farmer?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: No, not me. Burntmeat stick to what he best at doing. But I got me an assistant wanting to learn farming. He called My Arm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your assistant's called WHAT?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: He called My Arm.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: My Arm.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yep, My Arm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But why is he called...

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: It a perfectly good troll name.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, enough! What do you want me to do for this Arm person?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: You gonna help My Arm grow goutweed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, you're a vicious monster who eats innocent people! Why should I ever help you again?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: See, [player name], if My Arm learns to grow goutweed, I get as much goutweed as I ever want. If dat happens, I promise you I never cook another human in my life.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Never. Burntmeat promises. Goat taste just as good as human if you add enough goutweed. Now you go talk to My Arm; he tell you what to do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have all that dragon stuff as my reward?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: What dragon stuff you talking 'bout?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When you were telling me about the man you cooked who talked about goutweed, there was loads of valuable stuff on the floor. Can I have it? PLEASE!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Sorry, Player - Burntmeat chucked dat stuff away. It no good to eat. Red metal clothes hurt troll teeth, and big whip thing get stuck in throat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gah! You're worse than the old man who wouldn't give me his hat!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: You go help My Arm now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll lend him a hand. But you'd better keep that promise about not cooking any more people!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're disgusting! I'm not helping you again.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Aww, now you done hurt Burntmeat's feelings.

sections.title: If the Burntmeat cutscene is interrupted

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: If you not paying attention, Burntmeat not going to talk to you!

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat after interrupting his cutscene

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were about to tell me about some job...

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Yep, I remember. See, when you come here asking me 'bout goutweed dat other time, I tell you how we trolls had picked it all, and it not grow no more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, you told me the only goutweed anywhere in the world was in your storage room.

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat after agreeing to help My Arm

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: You get on with helping My Arm, yah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, I'll do it.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Before I start helping you with the whole farming thing, could you just explain why you're called My Arm?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat's easy! You know we trolls named after der first thing wot we try to eat when we young?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so what did you try to eat?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: It were my dad's arm. I remember it look so big holding me, I just gotta give it a chew. So he done named me My Arm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And no one thinks it's a silly name?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Well, I seen worse. Most humans come through here, dey got names with like all numbers and things wot aren't even words! I met some [man/woman] earlier wot called [himself/herself] [other player's name].

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Well, I seen worse. Most humans come through here, dey got names with like all numbers and things wot aren't even words!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, okay, I'm sorry! (snicker)

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: If you happy 'bout my name now, I tell you 'bout dis farming job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, My Arm (snicker snicker), tell me about it.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Okieday. Now, I heard 'bout dese gouty thingies before. Da cooks up on Death Plateau were saying dey cooked a man wot had a lump in his pocket dat tasted like goutweed, but wasn't goutweed.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm is thinking this is da thingy we need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do we do about it?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You go to Death Plateau and search da big cooking pot for da goutweedy lump!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no need for that. I already have a tuber. Do you want to see it?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Just do as I say, yah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But there's no point in worrying about some weird lump on Death Plateau when I've got a tuber.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I not want to see your tuber! My Arm is organised troll who gets one thing done at a time. I need da goutweedy lump from da cooking pot on Death Plateau.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmph.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do I need to go there? Can't I just get you a proper gout tuber?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Never mind tubers. You fetch me da goutweedy lump from da cooking pot of Dung 'n' Ash on Death Plateau.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmph.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Go see Burntmeat - he give good directions for getting there.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm again

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You got da goutweedy lump yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You go getting yourself a move on, yah? My Arm getting bored. Ask Burntmeat if you needin' help findin' da place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it you wanted?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You is a bit forgetful. If you like, My Arm give you nice tap on head to clear out cobbiewebs from brain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No! Just tell me what it was you wanted.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Okieday, maybe some other time. Now, what you gotta do is go to Death Plateau and fetch me da goutweedy lump from da big cooking pot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, how could I forget?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Perhaps you is a bit thick in da head. Ask Burntmeat if you needin' help findin' da place.

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat after talking to My Arm

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: How you an' My Arm getting on with da goutweed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He wants me to fetch something from a cauldron on Death Plateau.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Death Plateau, huh? Dat's quite a long way. Do you know how to get dere?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am a navigational genius; I'll be fine.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Okieday. Just you mind out for dem troll cooks on Death Plateau - dey not as friendly as Burntmeat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This place is a maze - got any advice?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Okieday, listen up an' Burntmeat tell you da best way to Death Plateau...

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: You go out da north door of my kitchen an' walk all da way down da corridor.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Turn right at da end, and go down da stairs to da prison where we keep meat fresh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A prison where you keep meat fresh?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Yup. Can't keep people in crates like goutweed, dey go all dead an' smelly. Burntmeat tried it.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Walk straight through da prison. Da back door of da Stronghold is at da south end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, so I've gone northwards up the corridor, down the stairs, then southwards through the prison.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Dat is correct. Now you gettin' outside...

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: When you outside, you turn left an' go long way south-east along path.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: At the end, you gotta turn left for Death Plateau. You got da special boots, so you gonna be okay on da rocks there.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: At the end, you gotta turn left for Death Plateau. But watch out for da tricky rocks - maybe you gonna have trouble climbing dem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get to Death Plateau if I can't climb the rocks?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Burntmeat heard where da humans going on Death Plateau wear funny boots to help climb rocks. Dey get da boots from little dwarf woman wot lives near da plateau. Turn right to go to her house.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, you've been remarkably helpful, considering that you're a horrible monster who eats innocent people.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Aww! You hurry up an' get My Arm what he wanting.

sections.title: Obtaining the goutweedy lump

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow - HOT!

sections.title: Attempting to scoop from the pot again
Talking to Burntmeat after obtaining the goutweedy lump

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: How you an' My Arm getting on with da goutweed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So far he's had me go to Death Plateau to fetch a weird lump from the cauldron there.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Da cooks on da plateau not as good as Burntmeat.

sections.title: Giving the goutweedy lump to My Arm

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You got da goutweedy lump yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have it. Here, take the thing.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Huh. It looking a bit tatty...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So are you.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: ... but it smelling like goutweed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You ate it!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yep, it tasting like goutweed too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you going to do now?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Well, now My Arm know dese goutweedy lump things exist, you help me grow one, yah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. Where do you need me to start?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm done tried farming before. I stick all kindsa things in da ground, but nothing ever grow, not even when I water 'em.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you plant?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I bin planting everythin', [player name]. Sticks, rocks, dead goats... you name it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And none of those things grew?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Nope, not even a smidgen. My Arm dug 'em up again to see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So did you try getting help from someone who knows about farming?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yah, My Arm found a nice farmer and ate him, but he not help much. He taste kinda boring too.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not very clever, are you?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My ol' mum say I clever enough. What you getting at?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, nothing. Maybe I should see where you've been doing all this farming?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Sure t'ing, Player. I show you...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Hurry up, <player name>!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dis is Troll Generals' cave.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Climb up 'ere.

sections.title: Interrupting the dialogue after giving My Arm the goutweedy lump

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You done good fetching dat goutweedy lump. My Arm very impressed.

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat after My Arm eats the goutweedy lump

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: How you an' My Arm getting on with da goutweed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So far he's had me fetch some kind of lump from a pot on Death Plateau. I can't imagine what he'll want next.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Huh... Let me know when we got goutweed growing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, whatever.

sections.title: Fertiliser
Talking to My Arm on the roof

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dis is my quiet place. No other trolls come up here. It where I bin trying to grow stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a bit windy up here.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat is good, cos you smellin' distinctly whiffy. It like you got all dung in your pockets or something. Or maybe you needing a wash.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wash more than enough!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Okieday, maybe My Arm imagining da smell.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yup, but My Arm not mind. We trolls not get blown around by wind like diddy little humans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you think you could grow things here?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I t'ink so, yah. I got book 'bout farming for you to read.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where in the world did YOU get a book?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat farmer I ate had a book with him. My Arm is hopin' you knows how to read.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Farmer Gricoller's Farming Manual?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm not know what in book, so you read it, yuh? Perhaps it tell you how to grow t'ings up on mountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll have a look inside.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm again before reading the manual

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You know how to read book I got off farmer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course I can read it! Just give me a moment.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: If you forget how to read, it not a problem. My Arm give you little tap on head and maybe you remember again, no trouble.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, that won't be necessary.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm after losing the manual
Talking to My Arm again after reading the manual

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You know how to read book I got off farmer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course; I've been reading it.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat's good. Is it any use?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a useful section about preparing soil for growing crops. For a mountain area, it says we need to mix three buckets of camel dung and seven buckets of supercompost into the soil.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Urgh. Dat's a lotta camel dung.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fortunately, I've got enough dung with me.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm not like to say so earlier, but you bin smellin' like you done a whoopsie.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks a lot!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any dung in the Stronghold I could use?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Why you think trolls keep dung in Stronghold? Do you keep dung in your house?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was worth a try.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You could go ask a camel for some dung. I bet dey got plenty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ask a camel for dung?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Best way to get somefing: find someone who got plenty of it wot they don't want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How handy - I've got an amulet that lets me talk to camels.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Clever human. Off you go now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How handy - I'm already carrying my Camulet.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Clever human. Off you go now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I guess it's time for me to dig that old Camulet out of the bank.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: That could help. Off you go now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If only I still had that Camulet I got in that weird temple in the desert...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Wot a shame; off you go now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, it'd be silly for me to ask a camel. I can't talk to camels. But I do remember getting some camel dung before, in some desert town once...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm is sure you'll find some way to get dung out of a camel. Now trot along.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm after being told to fetch camel dung

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You put all dat dung and stuff in da soil yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not yet. I'm working on it.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You be getting move on, [player name].

sections.title: Preparing the patch
Using supercompost on the patch without a spade

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a spade to dig the supercompost in properly.

sections.title: Using ugthanki dung on the patch without a spade

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not mixing dung into the soil with my hands! I need a spade to dig it in properly.

sections.title: Using normal compost or ultracompost on the patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unfortunately the book definitely said SUPERcompost.

sections.title: Using an irrelevant item on the patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think that's going to help just now.

sections.title: Adding the last supercompost to the patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, that's enough supercompost.

sections.title: Adding more supercompost than needed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think it'll need any more supercompost.

sections.title: Adding the last ugthanki dung to the patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew - that's enough dung.

sections.title: Adding more ugthanki dung than needed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've given it enough dung already.

sections.title: Using an item on the patch after adding enough compost and dung

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My Arm can tend the patch himself. I'm not growing the goutweed for him.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm after treating the patch

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finished treating that soil patch for you.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Good, good. Always I bin wanting a farming patch to grow things in. Da soggy bits south of here, they not work at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do we need to do now?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Now we do like dat man say to Burntmeat. We gotta go to Tai Bwo Wannai Village and see Murcaily 'bout growing goutweed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We don't need to go there. I've got a gout tuber.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I not wanna be gettin' confused with your tuber. Man tell Burntmeat we need to go see Murcaily to grow goutweed, so dat where we going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You really need to be more flexible.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Trolls not flexible creatures. Humans too flexible; dey got little necks wot snap easy as anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a long way to Tai Bwo Wannai Village. We're going to need a boat...

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: What in the world is that THING?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is My Arm. We'd like to go to Karamja.

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: This is your what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's a troll. His name is My Arm.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yup, My Arm.

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: Whose arm?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind that now, we just want to go to Karamja. Can you take us?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: It was my dad's arm.

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: What about your dad's arm?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm tried to eat it.

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: Your arm did what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will you take us to Karamja?

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: I don't think I want the mad troll.

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: Or his arm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Either you can take us to Karamja, or you can discuss it with My Arm.

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: Oh no, not your arm too...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll take us?

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: Alright, just get the arm thing onto the ship.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Urrrgh!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm not liking dis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong with your ar... What's wrong with you?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You ever hear stories of troll sailors or troll pirates?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... can't think of any just now.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat 'cos trolls don't like da sea. We kinda sink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I've met some trolls that lived in the sea. There's a Fishing Colony up in the north-west.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't there... river trolls and sea trolls and things? They seem happy enough in the sea.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Them not proper trolls. Proper trolls live in mountains.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But they look just like land trolls!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: We trolls. Them not trolls. 'Nuff said.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, please yourself!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Are we dere yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No! Look - no land.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: How 'bout now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still. No. Land.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You gettin' angry with My Arm, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, no.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Is it 'cos I is thick?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not angry with you.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Oh, right.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Are we dere now?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Urrrgh! My Arm feeling sick...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: We will meet at dat village, yah?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, he's gone. I hope he knows the way to Tai Bwo Wannai Village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll come back another time.

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: But... that thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, he'll be fine just standing here.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm at Ardougne docks

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: What we waitin' for here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have to ask Captain Barnaby to let us onto his ship.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Okieday, My Arm gonna let you do the talking. He not seem to like me standin' here.

sections.title: Periodically while sailing

lines.speaker: Captain Barnaby
lines.text: Aaaaargh!

sections.title: Karamja
Talking to My Arm outside Tai Bwo Wannai

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: So dis is Tai Bwo Wannai Village, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, that's right. I've been here before when the villagers thought their village was cursed. I had to fetch some special herbs for their shaman.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You doin' a lot of favours for people. Is dere anything in it for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I usually get something. Except that time when Burntmeat made me fetch him a 'tasty human' for his stew. All he gave me was some burnt meat.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You not like Burntmeat's cooking?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Believe it or not, I didn't like it at all.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat a pity. He planning to give you more burnt meat when you done helpin' me. Maybe My Arm think of something better for you. But first you go find Murcaily - he gotta help us grow goutweed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Murcaily, right.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm again

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You done spoken to Murcaily 'bout goutweed yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get around to it.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You slow. My Arm gettin' bored.

sections.title: Talking to Murcaily before talking to My Arm

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Is there anything I can do for you, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you seen My Arm around here?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Umm... Bwana... I can see both of them. They are attached to your shoulders, yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Oh, forget I said that! Have you seen a troll around here?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: I do not know what one of those is, Bwana, but I am sure there are none in our village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dratted creature - where can he be? He was meant to be coming here.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Well, Bwana, when I am looking for someone, I find it helps to think about where they may be. If your friend was travelling this way, he is surely between here and the place where you last saw him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's see... I saw him in Brimhaven, but he ran off. He was coming this way to your village.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Then perhaps you should look for your friend along the route to Brimhaven.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

sections.title: Talking to Murcaily after talking to My Arm

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Is there anything I can do for you, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A troll called My Arm wants a favour from you. He wants to grow goutweed and he thinks you can help.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: What's a troll? And what was that about your arm, Bwana?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really don't want to discuss it. Can you help this creature grow goutweed?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Goutweed? People grow it in farming patches all the time. Have you tried planting a gout tuber in your patch?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, the silly troll won't listen. He had me bring him all the way down from the mountains to ask you about it.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: You want to grow it in the mountains, Bwana? That might be more difficult. Gout tubers only grow well in warm places with plenty of sun.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean we aren't going to be able to grow it in the mountains?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: No, not with a normal gout tuber. Those just won't survive the conditions. You'll need a special hardy gout tuber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, wonderful. Can you give me some of those, please?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Oh no, Bwana. Those are far too valuable to give away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You owe me a bit of favour for the work I've been doing around the village. Are you sure you can't let me have some hardy gout tubers for the troll?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Oh, I suppose I could let you have one. But only because you've got over sixty percent favour. If we didn't owe you so much for your help, I'd not be giving out hardy gout tubers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this hardy tuber will grow like any other herb seed?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: It grows much faster than any other I have seen, Bwana. But remember, although it is hardy enough to grow in the cold mountain lands, it may still become diseased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on. I can't hold any more stuff just now. I'll get back to you in a minute.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: You are wise, Bwana. I have seen many an adventurer accidentally drop valuable items on the floor through trying to hold too many things.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm bored...

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: What is this noise?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Where's da goutweed?

lines.speaker: Qutiedoll
lines.text: Agh - a troll!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Want goutweed!

lines.speaker: Qutiedoll
lines.text: Eek!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm want goutweed!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Be careful!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: A broodoo man!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Has you got goutweed?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Grrr!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Still want goutweed!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Jagdakobo - here is the monster!

lines.speaker: Jagdakobo
lines.text: Prepare to die!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Where you keep goutweed?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You got issues!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Humans all mental.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Make it go away!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: I'll give you anything!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm want goutweed!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Okay, here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the heck was all THAT about?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You take far too long chattin' with Murcaily. My Arm come in to hurry things up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You could have really damaged their village!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Silly humans got such flimsy homes. One good thump an' der walls come down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you ever come to [location of player-owned house], I'm not letting you into my house.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Anyway, you got da goutweedy stuff, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I've got plenty of hardy gout tubers.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Right. Let's get back to da ship an' go back to Ardougne. My Arm fink you need to go to da bank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think I need from the bank?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: See, [player name], My Arm done tried planting stuff before, an' da birds around Trollheim Mountain, dey got... interested.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The birds got interested in watching your farm?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yuh, an' dere was a great BIG one wot started trying to eat whatever I planted.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Exactly how BIG was this bird?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: It pretty BIG. You might wanna get some food an' stuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are we talking bigger than... a pigeon?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: How 'bout we just go back to Ardougne so you can get your fightin' stuff...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat captain didn't look very happy to see us again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, he wasn't.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: What was dat you said to him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I told him he'd better take us back, or else...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Or else what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Or else I'd let my arm loose on him.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: So... are we dere yet?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You probably wanna go to bank now to fetch yer fightin' stuff. When you finished, go back to da Troll Stronghold. My Arm gonna be waitin' by our farming patch on da roof.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you going to get back to the Troll Stronghold?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat not your problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll see you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I seem to have thrown it away.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You is dumber than a spotted kebbit! Go back an' ask Murcaily for some more!

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Oh, I hadn't thought of that...

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: No, Bwana, I'm very sorry, but you'll need to do a bit more work for us before we'll be able to spare you any hardy gout tubers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Roughly how much more favour do I need?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Let me see... I think if you get it up to 60 percent, I'd be able to let you have a hardy gout tuber for your friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you'd accept trading sticks instead?

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Sorry, Bwana, but I don't need those. I get plenty of trading sticks from all the people wanting to use my hardwood grove. Hardy gout tubers are too precious to be bought like that.

sections.title: Talking to Murcaily again after previously lacking favour or inventory space

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was asking you about hardy goutweed...

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Yes, Bwana, I remember. You wanted a hardy gout tuber so your friend could grow goutweed on his mountain. But you only have [0-59] percent favour in our village. Come back to me with at least 60 percent favour and I will let you have what you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Murcaily
lines.text: Yes, Bwana, I remember. We owe you big-time for the work you done in Tai Bwo Wannai Village, so I can let you have a hardy gout tuber for your friend to grow on his mountain.

sections.title: The Rocs
Talking to My Arm at the patch

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Ah, dulce domum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you say?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: It mean somefing like 'Home sweet home' in some old language. My Arm is well ed-oo-cated troll, not some no-brain rockhead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Riiight. Shall we get on with growing the goutweed now?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yup, dat sound good. You show me how to do dat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll hand you what you need, and you can use it on the patch.

sections.title: Giving My Arm farming items
Talking to My Arm before weeding

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: How's my farming coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, you've got a nice crop of weeds.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I not go to all dis trouble to grow weedies! You supposed to help me grow goutweed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, we'll get to that in a bit.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You give me what stuff I need to use on da patch and I use it. And hurry up, My Arm gettin' bored.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We should start by raking it.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Okieday, you gimme a rake an' My Arm do it.

sections.title: Giving seed dibber before weeding

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I can't get da goutweed thing in da ground with all these weeds here.

sections.title: Plant cure before weeding

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Da weedies look healthy enough, t'anks.

sections.title: Giving spade before weeding

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Da spade not working on dese weedies.

sections.title: Giving an irrelevant item

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I not sure what you want me to do with dat.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, forget it.

sections.title: Giving banknotes

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I not want your bitty bit of paper.

sections.title: Giving compost after the patch is already treated

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I t'ink we already treated da soil.

sections.title: Giving rake head

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat lookin' a bit short!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... there should be more of it than that. Sorry.

sections.title: Giving rake handle

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: What you t'ink I want with a stick, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... there should be more of it than that. Sorry.

sections.title: Attaching the rake handle and head
Talking to My Arm after weeding

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: How's my farming coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The patch is free from weeds, so we could plant a hardy tuber in it.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Already?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I suppose we could treat the soil with compost first.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: After dat you probably wanna give me da hardy gout tuber thingies, an' maybe somefing to poke 'em into da soil.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Also, I got plenty of da hardy gout tuber thingies, but I gonna need somefing to poke 'em into da soil.

sections.title: Giving rake or spade after weeding

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Nuffing' interesting happened.

sections.title: Giving seed dibber before tubers

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Don't I need somefing to plant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yes, you'll need the gout tubers.

sections.title: Giving hardy gout tubers

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: T'anks, [player name].

sections.title: After My Arm plants the gout tubers

lines.speaker: Drunken dwarf
lines.text: 'Ello, matey!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You a dwarf.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You not my matey!

lines.speaker: Drunken dwarf
lines.text: Aaaargh - matey!

lines.speaker: Unnamed troll child
lines.text: Ooh. Food for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aww, poor dwarfie. But he's sure to turn up again one of these days - drunken dwarves always keep coming back...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Uh-oh - I fink da birds noticed us farming...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Akka akka!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Save da goutweed!

lines.speaker: Tool leprechaun
lines.text: Heheh - ickle birdie!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Akka!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're worried about that?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: It gonna eat da goutweed - kill it, quick!

sections.title: After killing the baby roc

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: T'anks for saving da goutweed, [player name]. But I fink dis other birdie not happy with you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What other birdie?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Dat birdie behind you.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm after defeating the giant roc

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Ah, lovely goutweed all grown up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep, you can harvest it now.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: I gonna need a spade.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I think I can arrange a spade for you.

sections.title: Giving My Arm an item other than a spade

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: T'ank you very much for da kind offer, but My Arm not really needin' one of those just now.

sections.title: Attempting to pick the goutweed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think My Arm should do the honours.

sections.title: Giving My Arm a spade

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: W00t - farming level!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: T'ank you so much for all da help! Burntmeat gonna be very pleased.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm just glad the goutweed grew up okay.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: My Arm just check dis da right stuff...

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yup, dis is goutweed. It lovverley.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't eat it all!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Aww, too late.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: You gotta go back to kitchen an' tell Burntmeat da good news. Maybe he give you nice reward for all da hard work you bin doin' for us. But if he not give you anyfing good, you come back an' see My Arm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll remember that.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm again before talking to Burntmeat

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Did Burntmeat give you nice reward?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I haven't asked him yet.

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: He downstairs in da kitchen. An' if he give you somefing rubbish, come tell My Arm. Maybe I can reward you better.

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We've done it! My Arm's managed to grow some goutweed on the roof of the Stronghold!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: So now we can grow goutweed whenever we want it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, he's got plenty of hardy gout tubers to grow in his little farming patch.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: T'ank you so much! I dunno what we'd have done if you not come through here to help us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... you mentioned a reward?

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Oh yup, Burntmeat cooked you SPECIAL reward.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: LOTS of burnt meat!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: See, Burntmeat promised you a reward! Anyway, it been nice to see you again, Player. Enjoy your meal.

sections.title: Talking to Burntmeat again

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Burntmeat hope you like your reward. It very special recipe Burntmeat learned when much younger troll.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, it was very... kind of you.

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Ah, you polite human. Off you go now and see what My Arm want.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was the worst quest reward EVER! I get better things from killing goblins! I get better things from killing SPIDERS! Why in the world did I ever do a quest for a troll? I could have been training my skills

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: instead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could have trained a cat and got a load of death runes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could have gone back to Pest Control and helped save the universe from hideous monsters!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But no, I did a quest for a troll, and what did I get?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: BURNT MEAT! What am I meant to do with burnt meat? I don't WANT burnt meat!

lines.speaker: Burntmeat
lines.text: Human so funny, pretending [he/she] don't like lovely burnt meat! Off you go now and see what My Arm wants.

sections.title: Talking to My Arm after talking to Burntmeat

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Did Burntmeat give you nice reward?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, he jolly well didn't. He gave me burnt meat. Again!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Aww, dat a shame. See, Burntmeat like burnt meat. It his favourite thing to cook, an' he do it better than any other troll.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said you might be able to give me something better?

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Yup, an' I will. My Arm gonna tell you big hushy-hushy secret about herbs...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha!

lines.speaker: My Arm
lines.text: Now you know more 'bout herbs. Also, I made a new farming patch like you showed me. Whatever you plant in it, I make sure it don't get disease.
